title: Hugo and the Missing Moon Rock
synopsis: Hugo discovers that the precious moon rock from his school's science museum has mysteriously disappeared. Using his detective skills and scientific knowledge, he searches for clues throughout the school to solve the mystery and find the missing space treasure.

pages.text: Hugo loved science class more than anything. Today was extra special because Mrs. Chen was showing the class a real moon rock! The small gray stone sat in a special glass case in the school's mini science museum. Hugo pressed his nose against the glass, amazed that this tiny rock had come from outer space.

illustration.description: Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes examining a glass display case in a cluttered science museum room filled with telescopes, planet models, fossils, magnifying glasses, test tubes, books, microscopes, star charts, and various scientific instruments scattered on shelves and tables
illustration.alt: Hugo looking at a moon rock display in a busy science museum classroom

pages.text: "This moon rock is 4.5 billion years old," Mrs. Chen explained. "It was brought back by astronauts on the Apollo mission." Hugo's eyes grew wide. He couldn't wait to tell his dad about it after school. But when lunch time ended and the class returned, something was terribly wrong.

illustration.description: A classroom scene with Hugo (straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes) and other children listening to Mrs. Chen, surrounded by space posters, planet mobiles hanging from ceiling, books about astronomy, toy rockets, astronaut helmets, space suits, lunar maps, and constellation charts covering every surface
illustration.alt: Mrs. Chen teaching Hugo and classmates about the moon rock in a space-themed classroom

pages.text: The glass case was empty! The precious moon rock had vanished. Mrs. Chen looked worried. "Oh no! The moon rock is gone!" she said. Hugo felt like a detective. He had read many mystery books, and now he had a real mystery to solve. "Don't worry, Mrs. Chen," Hugo said bravely. "I'll find it!"

illustration.description: Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes standing next to an empty glass display case, with Mrs. Chen looking concerned, in a room packed with detective tools like magnifying glasses, fingerprint powder, measuring tape, notebooks, pencils, evidence bags, mystery books, and scientific equipment scattered everywhere
illustration.alt: Hugo volunteering to solve the mystery of the missing moon rock

pages.text: Hugo got out his special magnifying glass that his grandpa had given him. He looked very carefully around the empty case. "Every good detective looks for clues," he said to himself. He spotted something interesting - tiny gray dust particles near the case!

illustration.description: Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes using a large magnifying glass to examine the area around the display case, surrounded by detective equipment including fingerprint kits, rulers, evidence markers, notebooks, pens, flashlights, tweezers, small bags, and scientific measuring tools scattered across tables and floor
illustration.alt: Hugo examining clues with his magnifying glass near the empty display case

pages.text: Hugo followed the trail of gray dust. It led from the science museum case, down the hallway, past the library, and straight to the janitor's closet! The dust sparkled a little bit in the light, just like moon rock dust might. Hugo's heart raced with excitement. He was getting closer to solving the mystery!

illustration.description: Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes crouched down following a trail of dust particles through a school hallway cluttered with backpacks, lunch boxes, art supplies, books, papers, pencils, erasers, rulers, science fair projects, posters, and various school equipment lining the walls and floor
illustration.alt: Hugo following a trail of dust through the busy school hallway

pages.text: At the janitor's closet, Hugo found more clues. There were muddy footprints and a small piece of paper with space stickers on it. Hugo knew those stickers! They were the same ones from the science museum display. Someone had definitely been here with the moon rock. But who could it be?

illustration.description: Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes examining muddy footprints and a paper with space stickers outside a janitor's closet, surrounded by cleaning supplies, mops, buckets, spray bottles, paper towels, brooms, dustpans, rubber gloves, sponges, and various maintenance tools organized in a cluttered but systematic way
illustration.alt: Hugo discovering footprints and space stickers as clues near the janitor's closet

pages.text: Hugo heard a soft crying sound coming from behind the gym. He followed the sound and found his classmate Emma sitting on the ground, looking very sad. Next to her was the missing moon rock! "Emma!" Hugo said gently. "Why did you take the moon rock?"

illustration.description: Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes approaching Emma who is sitting sadly next to the moon rock, in an area behind the gym filled with sports equipment including basketballs, soccer balls, jump ropes, cones, nets, whistles, water bottles, towels, first aid kits, and various athletic gear scattered around
illustration.alt: Hugo finding Emma with the missing moon rock behind the gym

pages.text: Emma looked up with tears in her eyes. "I wanted to show my little brother the moon rock," she said. "He loves space, but he's sick and couldn't come to school today. I was going to bring it right back, I promise!" Hugo felt sorry for Emma. He understood why she took it, even though it was wrong.

illustration.description: Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes comforting Emma, with the moon rock between them, surrounded by a playground area packed with swings, slides, monkey bars, sandbox toys, chalk, jump ropes, balls, frisbees, picnic tables, and playground equipment creating a busy but organized scene
illustration.alt: Hugo listening to Emma explain why she took the moon rock

pages.text: "I have an idea," Hugo said kindly. "Let's ask Mrs. Chen if we can take a photo of the moon rock for your brother. That way he can see it without us taking it from the museum." Emma smiled through her tears. "That's a great idea, Hugo! Thank you for understanding." Together, they returned the moon rock to its proper place, and Mrs. Chen was so proud of Hugo for solving the mystery with kindness.

illustration.description: Hugo with straight light blonde hair, fair skin, light blue eyes and Emma walking together carrying the moon rock back to the science museum, through a hallway filled with student artwork, science fair projects, bulletin boards, lockers, backpacks, books, pencils, papers, folders, and educational posters covering every available surface
illustration.alt: Hugo and Emma returning the moon rock together to the science museum

metadata.illustrationStyle: detailed hidden objects, visual clues, busy organized composition, search and find, detailed environment, many small objects, magnifying glass perspective, cluttered room or scene

questions.question: What was special about the moon rock in Hugo's science class?
choices: It could glow in the dark
questions.explanation: Mrs. Chen explained that the moon rock was 4.5 billion years old and was brought back by astronauts on the Apollo mission.

questions.question: What tool did Hugo use to look for clues?
questions.explanation: Hugo used his special magnifying glass that his grandpa had given him to examine the area around the empty case for clues.

questions.question: What clue helped Hugo follow the trail?
questions.explanation: Hugo spotted tiny gray dust particles near the case that sparkled in the light, and he followed this trail to find more clues.

questions.question: Why did Emma take the moon rock?
choices: She wanted to keep it forever
She wanted to show her sick brother
questions.explanation: Emma took the moon rock because she wanted to show it to her little brother who loves space but was sick and couldn't come to school.

questions.question: How did Hugo solve the problem kindly?
choices: He told the teacher on Emma
He suggested taking a photo for her brother
He let Emma keep the rock
questions.explanation: Hugo kindly suggested they ask Mrs. Chen if they could take a photo of the moon rock for Emma's brother, so he could see it without taking it from the museum.
